chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.1821 of 2023 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of bail application 21.9.2023 Syed Fazal-ur-Rehman advocate for the applicant alongwith applicant Abdul Rehman Mr. Muntazir Mehdi, Additional PG IO/Inspector Asma Bano, PS Awami Colony Karachi Complainant Mst. Saima is present in person ------------------------- The applicant / accused Abdul Rehman has been booked in Crime No.391/2023, registered against him under Section 376/443/34 PPC at PS Awami Colony Karachi. 2. Briefly the facts of the case are that the complainant has implicated the accused Abdul Rehman in the commission of offence under Section 376/443/34 PPC. 3. At the very outset, the complainant Mst. Saima, who is present in person, agreed to the disposal of this bail application on the terms that she has no grievance against the applicant/accused as such she has no objection, if the interim bail granted to the applicant vide order dated 18.8.2023 is confirmed. In such a situation, learned counsel for the applicant prays for confirmation of the interim bail granted to the applicant vide order dated 18.8.2023. 4. Without prejudice to the right of the parties at trial, learned APG has endorsed the point of view of the learned counsel for the applicant and complainant. 5. I have heard the learned counsel for the parties and have perused the material available on record. 6. Keeping in view the consensus arrived between the parties, this Court is of the tentative view that the applicant has been attending the trial Court and the parties have settled their dispute outside the Court though the alleged offense is non-compoundable, therefore, no fruitful purpose will be served to send the applicant behind the bars at this stage when the parties have buried the hatchet as per their statement more particularly the statement of the complainant, who is present in person and states that she has no grievance against the applicant at all so far as the allegations leveled against him in the FIR are concerned,

I have heard the learned counsel for the parties and have perused the material available on record. 6. Keeping in view the consensus arrived between the parties, this Court is of the tentative view that the applicant has been attending the trial Court and the parties have settled their dispute outside the Court though the alleged offense is non-compoundable, therefore, no fruitful purpose will be served to send the applicant behind the bars at this stage when the parties have buried the hatchet as per their statement more particularly the statement of the complainant, who is present in person and states that she has no grievance against the applicant at all so far as the allegations leveled against him in the FIR are concerned, 2 as such the interim bail granted to the applicant vide order dated 18.8.2023 is hereby confirmed on the same terms and conditions. 7. It is made clear that nothing stated or observed while deciding the instant bail application shall be tantamount to expression on the merits of the case. However, the learned trial Court is directed to conclude the trial of the case within one month after receipt of this order, more particularly the complainant must be examined. 8. This bail application stands disposed of. JUDGE Zahid/* >> 3 4
